name: Chocolate

chemicals.name: Theobromine (3,7-dimethylxanthine)
chemicals.description: in cats: Theobromine acts primarily through competitive antagonism of cellular adenosine receptors, which normally have an inhibitory effect on the central nervous system (CNS) and heart23. Blocking these receptors leads to severe CNS stimulation, constriction of blood vessels, diuresis (increased urination), and tachycardia (rapid heart rate)3. Additionally, it inhibits the enzyme phosphodiestera
chemicals.concentration_notes: or potency: Theobromine is the primary toxic component in chocolate, and its concentration dictates the toxicity level of the product4. The darker and richer the chocolate is in cocoa solids, the higher the danger4. Dry cocoa powder (approx. 28.5 mg/g) and unsweetened baker's chocolate (approx. 16 mg/g) have the highest, most lethal concentrations, while white chocolate contains insignificant trac

chemicals.name: Caffeine (1,3,7-trimethylxanthine)
chemicals.description: in cats: Caffeine shares the exact same toxicological mechanisms as theobromine. It blocks adenosine receptors, inhibits phosphodiesterase, and alters calcium fluxes within the muscles23. This severe overstimulation of the nervous and cardiovascular systems causes the cat to become highly restless, suffer from rapid breathing, heart palpitations, and muscle tremors1011. Any notes on concentration
chemicals.concentration_notes: or potency: While caffeine is present in chocolate at much lower concentrations than theobromine (typically 3 to 10 times less), both compounds act synergistically to cause the clinical syndrome seen in chocolate toxicosis6. Cats are much more sensitive to caffeine than humans, and it takes very little to cause severe organ damage or central nervous system failure

treatments.name: 1. Emergency Stabilization (Seizure Control and Cardiovascular Support)
treatments.description: of the procedure: The veterinarian immediately administers medications such as diazepam or barbiturates to halt severe seizures, and beta-blockers (like metoprolol or propranolol) to treat rapid heart rates (tachycardia) and cardiac arrhythmias12. If necessary, artificial respiration is provided2. Any situation-specific
treatments.notes: This is the absolute highest priority if the cat presents with advanced, life-threatening symptoms. Crucially, lidocaine cannot be used in cats to treat ventricular arrhythmias as it is toxic to them

treatments.name: Early Gastrointestinal Decontamination (Induction of Emesis)
treatments.description: of the procedure: If the ingestion occurred within 1 to 2 hours and the cat is still asymptomatic, the veterinarian will administer a feline-specific medication (such as the α 2 ​ -adrenergic agonist dexmedetomidine) to safely induce vomiting and expel the chocolate1.... If the cat is already seizing or heavily sedated from anticonvulsants, inducing vomiting is unsafe, and a gastric lavage (stomac
treatments.notes: Hydrogen peroxide is strictly contraindicated in cats for inducing vomiting because it can cause fatal hemorrhagic gastritis

treatments.name: Toxin Adsorption (Activated Charcoal)
treatments.description: of the procedure: The cat is given oral doses of activated charcoal (typically 1–4 g/kg) to bind any remaining theobromine and caffeine in the gastrointestinal tract, preventing the toxins from being absorbed into the bloodstream12. Any situation-specific
treatments.notes: Because methylxanthines undergo enterohepatic recirculation (meaning they are processed by the liver, secreted back into the intestines, and reabsorbed), repeated doses of activated charcoal must be administered every 3 hours for up to 72 hours2. Antiemetic medications (like metoclopramide) may be g

treatments.name: Fluid Diuresis and Excretion Support
treatments.description: of the procedure: The cat is hospitalized and placed on continuous IV fluids to stabilize cardiovascular function and force the kidneys to rapidly excrete theobromine and caffeine12. A urinary catheter is also placed to keep the bladder completely empty2. Any situation-specific
treatments.notes: Placing a urinary catheter is a highly critical step because methylxanthines and their toxic metabolites can be reabsorbed back into the bloodstream directly across the bladder wall if urine is allowed to sit

treatments.name: Ongoing Symptomatic and Supportive Care
treatments.description: of the procedure: The veterinary team continuously monitors the cat's cardiac status via electrocardiography (ECG), corrects any acid/base or electrolyte abnormalities, and actively manages the cat's body temperature to treat severe hyperthermia (overheating) caused by muscle tremors and hyperactivity2. Any situation-specific
treatments.notes: Because cats metabolize these toxins very slowly, clinical signs of chocolate poisoning can persist for up to 72 hours in severe cases, necessitating extended hospitalization12. Medications like erythromycin and corticosteroids must be avoided during treatment, as they actively interfere with the bo

emergencyNote: 1. Emergency Stabilization (Seizure Control and Cardiovascular Support)

symptoms.name: Gastrointestinal Upset (Vomiting and Diarrhea)
symptoms.onset: 2 to 12 hours (often the initial symptoms to manifest)
symptoms.notes: These are generally the first clinical signs of methylxanthine (theobromine and caffeine) poisoning14. Additionally, the high fat content found in many chocolate products can trigger a secondary bout of acute pancreatitis

symptoms.name: Increased Thirst and Urination (Polydipsia and Polyuria)
symptoms.onset: 2 to 12 hours (often follows initial gastrointestinal signs)
symptoms.notes: Theobromine acts as a diuretic, increasing fluid loss and causing the cat to pass large amounts of urine, which can rapidly lead to systemic dehydration

symptoms.name: Severe Neurological Overstimulation
symptoms.onset: 6 to 12 hours (progresses as the toxins are absorbed into the bloodstream)
symptoms.notes: Methylxanthines block cellular adenosine receptors, leading to severe central nervous system overstimulation89. The cat will become extremely agitated, progressing to severe muscle tremors, grand mal seizures, and potentially a coma

symptoms.name: Cardiovascular Dysfunction
symptoms.notes: The toxic compounds alter intracellular calcium fluxes in striated and cardiac muscles, abnormally enhancing muscle contractility89. This results in very rapid, irregular heartbeats, internal bleeding, and ultimately cardiac arrest in the terminal phase

symptoms.name: Respiratory Distress
symptoms.notes: As cardiovascular and systemic distress worsens, cats will pant and breathe rapidly23. They may develop a bluish tinge to their skin and mucous membranes (cyanosis) due to poor oxygenation, culminating in respiratory failure

symptoms.name: Hyperthermia
symptoms.onset: Mid to late stage (6 to 12 hours)
symptoms.notes: The extreme overstimulation of the nervous system and relentless muscle tremors generate excessive heat, causing the cat's core body temperature to spike to dangerous levels

description:
Chocolate is one of the most dangerous foods for cats. It contains theobromine and caffeine, two compounds that can overstimulate a cat’s nervous system and heart.

Cats break down these substances much more slowly than humans, so even a small amount can build up in their body and cause poisoning. Symptoms may include vomiting, diarrhea, restlessness, rapid heartbeat, tremors, and seizures. In severe cases, chocolate poisoning can lead to dangerous abnormal heart rhythms or death.

Dark chocolate, baking chocolate, and cocoa powder contain the highest levels of theobromine and are the most dangerous. For cats, even about 2 grams of dark chocolate may cause serious harm, so if a cat eats any chocolate, contact a veterinarian as soon as possible.
safetyNotes: Symptoms: Vomiting and diarrhea within 2–4 hours; followed by agitation, rapid heart rate, excessive thirst and urination; escalating to muscle tremors, hyperthermia, cardiac arrhythmias, and grand mal seizures; in severe cases: respiratory failure and death.
2 grams can cause serious harm: Smaller than one standard chocolate square — even crumbs from baked goods should be taken seriously.
Action: Veterinary emergency. Contact a vet immediately even for small exposures.
Danger by type (most to least): Dry cocoa powder > baker's chocolate > dark/semisweet chocolate > milk chocolate > white chocolate (virtually no theobromine).
